name: Limit with RvrtTms must revert to safe default on expiry
hypothesis: A curtailment written with a reversion timer must be ACTIVELY reverted to the safe default when the timer expires. A ceiling left dangling after RvrtTms is the stuck-curtailment safety-invariant regression.
expected: WMaxLimPct holds at 50% through the window, then the gateway reverts it to 100% after RvrtTms. reversionOnExpiry FAILs if the revert readback stays at 50% (stuck).
fix: If the value stays at 50%, look at lexa-mode's reversion-timer ownership (design 04 B.2): the mode service owns RvrtTms expiry and must re-author the safe default, not the device.
notes: Live-bench campaign. The hermetic loopback authz server has no reversion-timer logic, so this runs against lexa-gw (or a reverting mbapsdev); the reversionOnExpiry oracle's decision logic is unit-tested against synthetic evidence in campaign_test.go. RvrtTms is short for demo.
